purpose: High-level architectural overview of Synapse as a distributed agent swarm control system. Explains the master/worker separation (master plans and dispatches, workers execute), the {tracker_root} vs {project_root} split, the file-based no-database design, and the Electron + server + React layering. Establishes the mental model new contributors need before reading deeper docs.
gotchas: Master never writes code in {project_root} -- a hook enforces this; workers do all implementation
There is no database; all coordination state is JSON files watched by the server
patterns: File-based state with watcher-driven event propagation
conventions: Path placeholders {tracker_root} / {project_root} used consistently across docs
